_source: Strategy memos / positioning doc / curation principles (Google Docs) — authored in-OS or synced via n8n

data.what_we_do: A nonprofit that convenes influential leaders across the AI ecosystem through dinners, salons, workshops, invite-only gatherings, and The Curve conference.
data.primary_asset: The network of relationships among influential people in the AI ecosystem.

nodes.title: The trusted convener of frontier-AI governance dialogue

data.differentiation: Small, high-trust, off-the-record rooms where frontier-lab leadership, policymakers, and researchers actually talk to each other.
data.desired_role: The default neutral ground for the hardest AI governance conversations.
signals_of_success: policymakers and labs in the same room
viewpoint diversity, not an echo chamber

nodes.title: Chatham House Rule by default; protect attendee trust above all

data.detail: No attribution without explicit consent. Journalists attend as participants, not reporters, unless agreed otherwise.

nodes.title: Every governance convening must hold genuine viewpoint balance

data.detail: No single lab, ideology, or policy stance should dominate the room. Safety, accelerationist, and policy-skeptic voices all belong.

nodes.title: Every invitation flows through a known relationship

data.detail: Cold invitations to senior figures damage trust. An invite needs a relationship owner who can make it personal.

nodes.title: Don't invite a frontier-lab principal or senior policymaker without a named relationship owner + Steve/Taren sign-off

data.condition: invitee seniority == principal AND community in (frontier-labs, policy)

nodes.title: Hold a viewpoint-balance check before any list is finalized

data.condition: attendee list finalized for a governance event
data.constraint: Network Gap Agent must clear viewpoint and community balance; imbalance escalates to the President

nodes.title: Never expose attendee lists or contact data outside the relationship owner without consent

data.condition: any attendee data leaves the OS
